title: Implement config system with global and per-repo YAML merge
description:
Implement config system with global and per-repo YAML merge. Scope includes:
- Config file: ~/.config/nexus/config.yml (global) + .nexus/config.yml (per-repo, merged at load)
- Environment variable overrides: CHROMA_API_KEY, VOYAGE_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, MXBAI_API_KEY, NX_PM_ARCHIVE_TTL, NX_ANSWER
- Config dataclass with strongly-typed fields (no YAML parsing in-code; see METHODOLOGY.md)
- CLI commands (in config_cmd.py): nx config show (print merged config as YAML), nx config set <key> <value> (write to global config)
- Config precedence: env vars > per-repo config.yml > global config.yml > defaults
- Tests: unit tests for merge logic, env override, and round-trip set/show

title: Fix SessionStart crashes on T2 unavailability instead of degrading gracefully
description: hooks.py:58-73: session_start() calls _open_t2(), db.get(), and db.list_entries() without error handling. If T2 is unavailable (disk full, SQLite corruption, first run before DB creation), the SessionStart hook raises an unhandled exception and blocks Claude Code from opening. Fix: wrap all T2 operations in a broad try/except; on failure, append a warning line to the output and continue. The hook should degrade gracefully: user gets a warning about memory unavailability but Claude Code opens normally.

title: nexus: [Fix E] Critical test coverage — AST languages, md integrity, PDF normalization, hash sync
description:
TDD foundation: Write 4 test files (tests 1-4 of 5) that have NO dependency on other fixes. These tests must be written FIRST per TDD discipline. The 5th test file (test_indexer_chunk_flow.py) depends on Fix A and is tracked separately in nexus-eae0.

Files to create:
1. tests/test_chunker_ast_languages.py — AST chunking correctness for Python, JS, Go, Rust, Java
2. tests/test_md_chunker_semantic_integrity.py — code block not split, list intact, table pipes preserved
3. tests/test_pdf_extractor_normalization.py — tabs, U+00A0, excess newlines, combined edge cases
4. tests/test_doc_indexer_hash_sync.py — unchanged file skips Voyage embed; modified file re-embeds

Pattern: use @pytest.mark.skipif(not TREE_SITTER_AVAILABLE, reason='...') guards (same pattern as existing tests). Stub Voyage AI with MagicMock, stub ChromaDB CloudClient with EphemeralClient or MagicMock. ~160 LOC total across 4 files. No new dependencies.

EXISTING TEST GAP CONFIRMED by audit:
- tests/test_chunker.py covers line fallback and basic AST mocking but has NO test that exercises real AST parsing for specific languages (Python, JS, Go, Rust, Java) with actual tree-sitter parsers
- tests/test_md_chunker.py covers heading/frontmatter/char-offset tests but has NO test for code block preservation or list/table structural integrity
- No existing test for PDF extractor normalization (tabs/U+00A0/excess newlines)
- No existing test for hash-based skip logic in the indexer pipeline

NOTE for test_md_chunker_semantic_integrity.py: Tests here drive nexus-n72s (Fix C2 — preserve_code_blocks). Write the tests first; they will FAIL until Fix C2 is implemented. That is correct TDD behavior.

References: RDR-015 Fix E, RDR-014 R7
notes:
## Implementation Details

### File 1: tests/test_chunker_ast_languages.py (~50 LOC)
Purpose: Verify AST chunking produces correct chunks for 5 languages.

```python
import pytest
from pathlib import Path

try:
    from tree_sitter_language_pack import get_parser
    TREE_SITTER_AVAILABLE = True
except ImportError:
    TREE_SITTER_AVAILABLE = False

from nexus.chunker import chunk_file

pytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(
    not TREE_SITTER_AVAILABLE,
    reason='tree-sitter-language-pack not available',
)

# Use real code snippets via tmp_path fixture
# Each test: write a file with known structure, call chunk_file(), assert:
#   - len(chunks) >= 2 (enough code to produce multiple AST chunks)
#   - all chunks have ast_chunked=True
#   - line_start/line_end are populated and 1-indexed
#   - text is non-empty
```

Test cases:
- test_python_ast_chunks: ~200-line Python file with 3 functions and a class
- test_javascript_ast_chunks: ~150-line JS file with 2 functions and a class
- test_go_ast_chunks: ~150-line Go file with 2 functions and a struct
- test_rust_ast_chunks: ~150-line Rust file with impl block and functions
- test_java_ast_chunks: ~200-line Java file with class, methods, inner class

### File 2: tests/test_md_chunker_semantic_integrity.py (~40 LOC)
Purpose: Verify SemanticMarkdownChunker preserves structural elements.

Test cases:
- test_code_block_not_split: Markdown with a fenced code block inside a section -> code block text appears in a single chunk, not split across chunks
- test_list_intact: Markdown with a bulleted list -> all list items appear in one chunk
- test_table_pipes_preserved: Markdown with a pipe table -> table rows preserved (pipe chars present)

### File 3: tests/test_pdf_extractor_normalization.py (~40 LOC)
Purpose: Verify PDF text normalization handles edge cases.

Import: from nexus.pdf_extractor import PDFExtractor (or the normalize function if exposed)

Test cases:
- test_tab_normalization: tabs -> spaces
- test_nbsp_normalization: U+00A0 -> regular space
- test_excess_newline_normalization: 3+ consecutive newlines -> 2 newlines
- test_combined_edge_cases: all above in one string

### File 4: tests/test_doc_indexer_hash_sync.py (~30 LOC)
Purpose: Verify content-hash-based skip/re-embed logic.

Test cases:
- test_unchanged_file_skips_embed: Mock Voyage + ChromaDB, index a file, index same file again -> second call skips embedding (mock.embed not called twice)
- test_modified_file_reembeds: Index a file, modify content, index again -> embed IS called the second time

Stubs: mock voyageai.Client.embed to return [[0.1] * 1024], use MagicMock for ChromaDB collection.get() to return matching content_hash on second call.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 test files exist in tests/
- [ ] pytest tests/test_chunker_ast_languages.py passes (at least 5 test functions)
- [ ] pytest tests/test_md_chunker_semantic_integrity.py passes (at least 3 test functions)
- [ ] pytest tests/test_pdf_extractor_normalization.py passes (at least 4 test functions)
- [ ] pytest tests/test_doc_indexer_hash_sync.py passes (at least 2 test functions)
- [ ] All tests skip cleanly when tree-sitter-language-pack unavailable (via pytest.mark.skipif)
- [ ] Full suite: pytest tests/ passes with no regressions
- [ ] No new dependencies added

## Gotchas
- chunker.py:AST_EXTENSIONS uses different language names than tree-sitter-language-pack for some languages (e.g. 'tsx' vs 'typescript'). Test with actual extensions.
- CodeSplitter line metadata may or may not include line_start/line_end depending on tree-sitter grammar. Test for presence, handle absence.
- PDF normalization may be in pdf_extractor.py or may need to be tested via the full extract() path. Check which function does the normalization before writing tests.

title: Fix logging bifurcation: server_main.py stdlib handler vs structlog in all other modules
description:
server_main.py:11-22 configures a stdlib RotatingFileHandler. All other modules use structlog. No bridge is configured. structlog records from polling.py, registry.py, server.py etc. may not route through the rotating file handler, depending on structlog processor chain configuration. Log formats are inconsistent (structlog key=value vs stdlib '%(asctime)s...').

Location: src/nexus/server_main.py:11-22

Fix: Configure structlog to emit to the RotatingFileHandler via structlog.configure() with stdlib integration (structlog.stdlib.LoggerFactory()). Or replace the explicit handler setup with a structlog-native file handler configuration.

title: Optimize frecency: replace O(N) per-file git log with single batch query
description: frecency.py:35-45 called from indexer.py:46: compute_frecency() spawns one git log --follow -- <file> subprocess per file. For a 5,000-file repo this is 5,000 subprocesses (~4 min at 50ms each) before any embedding. Fix: replace with a single 'git log --format=%ct --name-only HEAD' traversal over the entire repo, parse the output to build a dict[str, list[float]] of timestamps per file path, then compute frecency from that dict. This reduces subprocess overhead from O(N) to O(1). SeaGOAT (the reference implementation) uses this batch pattern. Update the frecency module API to accept the pre-built timestamp dict, or batch internally.

title: Fix _apply_env_overrides crashing on invalid env var value with no useful error
description:
If NX_SERVER_PORT=abc is set, cast(raw) calls int('abc') which raises ValueError with no indication of which env var caused it. This propagates through load_config() and crashes any subcommand that loads configuration.

Location: src/nexus/config.py (_apply_env_overrides section)

Fix: Wrap each cast(raw) call in try/except (ValueError, TypeError): re-raise as ValueError(f'Invalid value for {env_var!r}: cannot convert {raw!r} to {cast.__name__}: {exc}'). This gives users an actionable error message identifying exactly which env var has the problem.

title: nexus: [Fix A] Port DEFINITION_TYPES + _extract_name + _extract_context from arcaneum
description:
Port the tree-sitter AST context extraction infrastructure from arcaneum into src/nexus/indexer.py. This is the prerequisite for RDR-014 Fix 1 (code context prefix injection).

IMPORTANT CORRECTION: _extract_context() does NOT exist in arcaneum. It must be written from scratch using arcaneum's DEFINITION_TYPES and _extract_name() as building blocks.

What to copy from arcaneum/src/arcaneum/indexing/fulltext/ast_extractor.py:
- DEFINITION_TYPES dict (lines 51-136) — 13 languages including Scala (which is in the dict): Python, JS, TS, Java, Go, Rust, C, C++, C#, Ruby, PHP, Swift, Kotlin, Scala (14 language entries in actual source)
- _extract_name_from_node(node) -> str — adapted from lines 366-386 (_extract_name method uses child_by_field_name('name'/'identifier') pattern and fallback to child iteration)

CORRECTION: _CLASS_SEMANTICS and _METHOD_SEMANTICS do NOT exist in arcaneum. They must be defined from scratch:
  _CLASS_SEMANTICS = frozenset({'class', 'interface', 'module'})
  _METHOD_SEMANTICS = frozenset({'function', 'method', 'decorated'})

CORRECTION: _extract_context() does NOT exist in arcaneum and must be authored new. Its contract:
  _extract_context(source: bytes, language: str, chunk_start_0idx: int, chunk_end_0idx: int) -> tuple[str, str]
  Returns (class_name, method_name) — empty strings if no context found.

Algorithm for _extract_context:
1. Get parser via get_parser(language) from tree_sitter_language_pack
2. Parse source bytes: tree = parser.parse(source)
3. Walk tree.root_node depth-first
4. Use node.start_point[0] and node.end_point[0] (0-indexed line numbers)
5. Prune subtrees where node.end_point[0] < chunk_start_0idx or node.start_point[0] > chunk_end_0idx
6. When a node.type is in DEFINITION_TYPES[language] and its range overlaps the chunk:
   - If the code_type maps via _CLASS_SEMANTICS -> record as innermost class
   - If the code_type maps via _METHOD_SEMANTICS -> record as innermost method
7. Return (class_name, method_name)
~100 LOC total in src/nexus/indexer.py, no new deps (tree-sitter-language-pack==0.7.1 already pinned).

Location in indexer.py: After line 52 (after _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE dict), before _git_metadata function (line 55).

References: RDR-015 Fix A, RDR-014 R7, R11

        'class_definition': 'class',
        'object_definition': 'class',
        'trait_definition': 'interface',
    },
}
```

2. Semantic type sets:
```python
_CLASS_SEMANTICS = frozenset({'class', 'interface', 'module'})
_METHOD_SEMANTICS = frozenset({'function', 'method', 'decorated'})
```

3. _extract_name_from_node function (~15 LOC):
```python
def _extract_name_from_node(node) -> str:
    for field in ('name', 'identifier'):
        child = node.child_by_field_name(field)
        if child:
            try:
                return child.text.decode('utf-8')
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, AttributeError):
                pass
    for child in node.children:
        if child.type in ('identifier', 'name'):
            try:
                return child.text.decode('utf-8')
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, AttributeError):
                pass
    return ''
```

4. _extract_context function (~35 LOC):
```python
def _extract_context(
    source: bytes,
    language: str,
    chunk_start_0idx: int,
    chunk_end_0idx: int,
) -> tuple[str, str]:
    # Returns (class_name, method_name) for the given 0-indexed line range.
    # Uses depth-first pre-order traversal with subtree pruning.
    # See RDR-014 for full implementation.
```

### Import needed:
Add at the call site (lazy import inside _index_code_file, not at module top):
```python
from tree_sitter_language_pack import get_parser
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DEFINITION_TYPES dict covers 13 languages (Python, JS, TS, Java, Go, Rust, C, C++, C#, Ruby, PHP, Swift, Kotlin) + Scala = 14
- [ ] _extract_name_from_node correctly extracts names using field API first, child scan fallback
- [ ] _extract_context returns ('', '') for unsupported languages
- [ ] _extract_context returns correct (class_name, method_name) for a Python class with methods
- [ ] _extract_context returns correct class_name with empty method_name when chunk spans multiple methods
- [ ] _extract_context handles inner/nested classes (returns innermost)
- [ ] No new imports at module level (tree-sitter import is lazy)
- [ ] pytest tests/ passes with no regressions

## Gotchas
- arcaneum's DEFINITION_TYPES has 13 entries but the RDR mentions 13+Scala=14 languages. Double-check Scala is included (it is in ast_extractor.py:130-135).
- The _extract_name function in arcaneum returns 'anonymous' for unknown names. In nexus, return '' instead (empty string is more useful for the prefix format — allows omitting Class/Method when empty).
- tree-sitter node.text returns bytes, not str. Always decode with .decode('utf-8') and handle UnicodeDecodeError.
- get_parser() may raise if language not in tree-sitter-language-pack. Wrap in try/except in _extract_context.
- node.start_point and node.end_point return (row, column) tuples where row is 0-indexed.

title: Integrate voyage-context-3 contextualized embeddings for docs__* and knowledge__* collections
description:
## Context

Voyage AI offers `voyage-context-3` (POST /v1/contextualizedembeddings), which embeds all chunks of a document *together* so each chunk is informed by its siblings. Standard `voyage-4` embeds each chunk in isolation — losing meaning at boundaries. Demonstrated improvement: answer chunk rank 8/10 → 1/1 in Voyage's financial filing benchmark.

**ChromaDB research docs**:
- research::voyage-contextualized-embeddings::2026-02-22 (initial analysis)
- research::voyage-cce-integration-strategy::2026-02-22 (deep integration research — primary reference)

---

## CRITICAL CORRECTIONS from deep research

1. **Asymmetric retrieval**: voyage-context-3 is for INDEXING only. Query time ALWAYS uses voyage-4 for ALL collections. The embedding spaces are aligned by Voyage AI's training — voyage-4 queries work correctly on CCE-indexed collections. corpus.py does NOT need to change the query model.

2. **Batching IS supported**: The outer list in contextualized_embed() CAN contain multiple documents (inner lists). Batch 4–8 PDFs per API call. 120K token limit is per inner list (per document), not per outer list.

3. **Orphaned chunk bug in current code**: doc_indexer.py does NOT delete old chunks before upsert. If a file shrinks (5 chunks → 3 chunks), chunks 3 and 4 become ghost entries that appear in search results. This bug must be fixed in this bead alongside CCE.

4. **CCE threshold**: Use CCE only when len(chunks) >= 2. Single-chunk entries get zero benefit — same output as standard embed but with added latency and cost.

5. **nx store single-put**: voyage-4 ONLY. CCE is for bulk indexers (nx index pdf/md) and pm archive (if >1 chunk). spec.md already specifies this correctly.

---

## Model facts
- Model: voyage-context-3 (indexing) + voyage-4 (querying — unchanged)
- API: POST https://api.voyageai.com/v1/contextualizedembeddings
- Limits: 120K tokens per inner list (per document), 1000 inner lists per call, 16K total chunks
- Dimensions: 2048 (same as voyage-4 — no schema change)
- No chunk overlap recommended (PDFChunker + SemanticMarkdownChunker already non-overlapping)
- Single-chunk inner lists behave identically to standard embed

### Python API
```python
# Batch multiple docs in one call (4-8 safe)
result = vo.contextualized_embed(
    inputs=[[doc1_chunk1, doc1_chunk2], [doc2_chunk1, doc2_chunk2, doc2_chunk3]],
    model="voyage-context-3",
    input_type="document",
)
# result.embeddings is a flat list — track offsets per doc
```

---

## Benefit by collection type

| Collection | Benefit | Why |
|---|---|---|
| docs__* | HIGH | PDFs + markdown lose cross-chunk context without CCE |
| knowledge__* | MODERATE | Long-form entries (>=2 chunks) benefit; standalone short entries unchanged |
| code__* | LOW / DEFER | No voyage-context-code model yet; frecency hybrid compensates |

---

## Scope: what this bead implements

### 1. Orphaned chunk fix (CRITICAL — must ship with CCE)
Add is_reindex flag + delete_by_source():

```python
# db/t3.py — new method
def delete_by_source(self, collection: str, source: str) -> None:
    """Delete all chunks with metadata.source == source."""\n    col = self._chroma.get_collection(name=collection)
    col.delete(where={"source": source})

# doc_indexer.py — index_pdf() and index_markdown()
def index_pdf(path: Path, ..., is_reindex: bool = False) -> int:
    ...
    if is_reindex:
        t3.delete_by_source(collection, str(path))  # prevent orphaned chunks
    col.upsert(ids=ids, documents=documents, embeddings=embeddings, metadatas=metadatas)
```

### 2. CCE embedding in doc_indexer.py (primary change)
After building chunks list, call contextualized_embed() when len(chunks) >= 2:

```python
MAX_CCE_TOKENS = 100_000  # conservative (hard limit 120K)

def _embed_with_fallback(vo, chunks, source, use_cce):
    estimated = sum(len(c) // 4 for c in chunks)
    if use_cce and estimated > MAX_CCE_TOKENS:
        log.warning("CCE token limit exceeded for %s, falling back to voyage-4", source)
        use_cce = False
    if use_cce:
        try:
            r = vo.contextualized_embed([chunks], model="voyage-context-3", input_type="document")
            return [e.embedding for e in r.embeddings], "voyage-context-3"
        except voyageai.error.InvalidRequestError as e:
            log.warning("CCE failed for %s: %s — falling back to voyage-4", source, e)
    r = _embed_with_retry(vo, chunks)
    return [e.embedding for e in r.embeddings], "voyage-4"
```

Pass pre-computed embeddings= to col.upsert() to bypass ChromaDB's VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction.
Update metadata: "embedding_model": "voyage-context-3" (or "voyage-4" on fallback).

### 3. Batching for bulk nx index operations
Collect multiple documents in outer list (batch_size=4 safe, 8 for small docs):

```python
def batch_index_docs(paths, collection, vo, t3, batch_size=4):
    all_chunks = [(path, chunks) for path in paths if (chunks := chunk_text(path))]
    for i in range(0, len(all_chunks), batch_size):
        batch = all_chunks[i:i+batch_size]
        result = vo.contextualized_embed(
            [chunks for _, chunks in batch], model="voyage-context-3", input_type="document"
        )
        offset = 0
        for (path, chunks) in batch:
            embs = [result.embeddings[offset+k].embedding for k in range(len(chunks))]
            offset += len(chunks)
            _upsert_doc(t3, collection, path, chunks, embs)
        time.sleep(0.5)  # rate limit guard
```

### 4. Two-phase collection migration guard
Mark collection metadata "migration_in_progress" before re-indexing; clear on completion. Guard at search time warns/errors on partial migration state.

### 5. Staleness check for model change
Add embedding_model to content-hash staleness check in doc_indexer.py so files indexed with voyage-4 are detected as stale and re-indexed when corpus.py returns voyage-context-3:

```python
if (existing_hash == content_hash
    and existing_metadata.get("embedding_model") == target_model):
    return 0  # already indexed with correct model
```

### 6. Failure recovery
- InvalidRequestError (token limit) → fallback to voyage-4 for that document
- RateLimitError → exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s), 5 retries
- AuthenticationError → abort entire operation
- Per HEAD polling loop: abort after 5 consecutive failures

### 7. output_dtype in collection metadata (future optimization)
Store output_dtype (default: "float") in collection metadata. At 50K+ chunks, int8 reduces storage 8x with ~1-3% recall loss. Do not ship int8 by default — add --dtype flag for future opt-in.

---

## Files to modify

### Primary: src/nexus/doc_indexer.py (spec lines ~28-178)
- index_pdf() and index_markdown(): add is_reindex flag, delete_by_source call, CCE embed path
- Add staleness check for embedding_model in content-hash guard
- Add batch_index_docs() helper for bulk nx index operations
- Token estimate pre-check + fallback to voyage-4

### Secondary: src/nexus/db/t3.py
- Add delete_by_source(collection, source) method
- Optional: add collection_metadata() accessor for migration guard

### Minor: src/nexus/corpus.py
- NO query model change needed (voyage-4 always used for queries)
- Add voyage-context-3 as the known index model for docs__* and knowledge__* (informational only)

### NOT needed: src/nexus/db/t3.py _embedding_fn() or src/nexus/search_engine.py
- Query embedding is unchanged (voyage-4 always used)
- Reranking and hybrid scoring unchanged

### DEFERRED: src/nexus/indexer.py (code__ collections)
- No voyage-context-code model yet; defer until Voyage releases one

---

## HEAD polling integration (src/nexus/polling.py)
- Pass is_reindex=True when calling index_repo on changed files
- Add 1s sleep between batches of 4 files
- Deleted files: call delete_by_source explicitly
- Error count limit: abort loop after 5 consecutive failures

---

## nx pm archive (src/nexus/pm.py)
- Use CCE only if len(archive_chunks) >= 2
- Spec already handles this correctly; implementation must match

---

## Rate limits
- Free: ~100-300K TPM; Standard: 2-5M TPM
- CCE billing: charged on total context (all chunks × full doc context) — more expensive than standard
- Full re-index 500 files: use batch_size=4, sleep=1.0s between batches
- Incremental HEAD polling (10-50 changed files): safe without sleep

---

## Success criteria
- [ ] delete_by_source() added to T3Client
- [ ] is_reindex flag in index_pdf() and index_markdown()
- [ ] CCE embed path active when len(chunks) >= 2 and is_reindex or fresh index
- [ ] Staleness check includes embedding_model field
- [ ] Batch outer list support for nx index (batch_size=4 default)
- [ ] Two-phase migration guard in collection metadata
- [ ] Failure recovery: token pre-check + InvalidRequestError fallback + RateLimitError backoff
- [ ] All existing 461 tests pass
- [ ] New tests: CCE called for multi-chunk docs; voyage-4 fallback for single-chunk and token overflow; delete_by_source called on re-index; orphaned chunks not returned after file shrinkage
- [ ] nx collection info shows voyage-context-3 as embedding_model for docs__* after re-index
- [ ] voyageai dependency confirmed in pyproject.toml

## Related beads
- nexus-gu8 (DONE): errors.py + types.py introduced
- nexus-3q6 (DONE): nx collection info shows embedding_model — reflects voyage-context-3 after re-index
- nexus-895 (DONE): search_engine.py refactor

## ChromaDB context
- research::voyage-contextualized-embeddings::2026-02-22 (initial CCE analysis)
- research::voyage-cce-integration-strategy::2026-02-22 (deep integration research — 10 Qs answered, risk matrix, code patterns)
- decision::nexus::cross-corpus-reranking (reranking feeds on better retrieval distances from CCE)
notes:
## Simplification: _embedding_fn() is always voyage-4

From architecture discussion (2026-02-22): Since CCE makes the query model uniform (voyage-4 for ALL collections at query time regardless of how they were indexed), and pre-computed embeddings bypass the per-collection embedding function at index time, the ChromaDB-stored embedding function becomes query-time-only and is voyage-4 everywhere.

### Implication for db/t3.py _embedding_fn()
Current code (corpus.py → t3.py):
  - code__*      → VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction('voyage-code-3')
  - docs__*      → VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction('voyage-4')
  - knowledge__* → VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction('voyage-4')

Simplified (CCE world):
  - ALL collections → VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction('voyage-4')
  - The stored ChromaDB embedding function is ONLY used for query-time embedding
  - Index-time model (voyage-context-3 or voyage-4 fallback) is tracked in metadata only

### What this eliminates
- corpus.py embedding_model_for_collection() no longer needs to drive _embedding_fn()
- The code__* special case in _embedding_fn() can be removed
- ChromaDB collection creation no longer needs per-prefix model logic
- Simplifies T3Database initialization significantly

### What this does NOT eliminate
- corpus.py embedding_model_for_collection() still useful for: (1) metadata tagging at index time, (2) nx collection info display, (3) staleness check (did this file get indexed with the right model?)
- code__* collections STILL use voyage-code-3 for INDEXING (via indexer.py) — only the query-time function simplifies
- The asymmetric nature of CCE (voyage-context-3 index, voyage-4 query) is the reason this works: voyage-4 is a valid query model for all collection types

### Implementation note
When creating or opening a ChromaDB collection, always pass VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction('voyage-4').
The index-time model is an implementation detail of the indexer, not a ChromaDB configuration.

title: Enhance nx collection info with embedding model and last-indexed fields

Actual implementation (commands/collection.py info_cmd) shows name and document count only — no embedding model, no last-indexed. (2) Last-indexed: query the collection for MAX(indexed_at) across all metadatas — do a collection.get(include=['metadatas']) and find the most recent indexed_at ISO timestamp. (3) Size: current count() serves this. title: Fix hidden-file filter uses absolute path parts — silently indexes nothing from dotted parent dirs
description: indexer.py:43: 'if any(part.startswith(".") for part in path.parts)' checks all components of the absolute path. If the repo lives at /home/user/.projects/myrepo, every file's parts include '.projects' and nothing gets indexed. Fix: check relative path only: path.relative_to(repo).parts. The existing test_run_index_skips_hidden_files uses a repo at tmp_path/repo where no parent starts with '.', so the bug is invisible. Add a test with a repo inside a dotted parent directory that verifies regular files ARE indexed while .git/ contents are NOT.

title: Fix missing-credentials silently marks repo ready with HEAD recorded — prevents retry
description: indexer.py:60-63 + polling.py:54-57: When VOYAGE_API_KEY or CHROMA_API_KEY is absent, _run_index() returns early without raising. index_repository() then sets status='ready'. check_and_reindex()'s finally block records the current head_hash regardless of whether indexing succeeded. Future polls see head_hash unchanged and skip re-indexing. Result: repo appears indexed with status='ready' but has zero embeddings; adding credentials later has no effect. Fix: either set status='error' / raise when credentials are absent, or don't update head_hash when indexing was skipped due to missing credentials. Add a test verifying this behavior.

title: Fix pm_status returning first-encountered agent instead of most-recently-updated
description:
pm.py:196-197: `if last_agent is None and row.get('agent')` sets last_agent to the first non-None value in an unordered iteration over all_rows. Spec requires the most-recently-written entry's agent.

Location: src/nexus/pm.py:196-197

Fix: Track (agent, timestamp) pairs: latest_ts = ''; for row in all_rows: ts = row.get('timestamp', ''); if row.get('agent') and ts > latest_ts: latest_ts = ts; last_agent = row['agent']. ISO 8601 timestamps compare correctly as strings.

title: Fix _sha256 reads entire file into memory — unbounded allocation for large PDFs
description: doc_indexer.py:16-17: _sha256(path) calls path.read_bytes() loading the entire file. A 200MB PDF is loaded into memory just to compute a hash. Fix: streaming hash with 64KB blocks: h = hashlib.sha256(); with path.open('rb') as f: for block in iter(lambda: f.read(65536), b''): h.update(block); return h.hexdigest(). This reduces memory usage from O(file_size) to O(1).

title: Implement nx store expire with guarded query for permanent entries
description:
WHAT: Implement TTL expiry for T3 knowledge__ chunks, with a guard ensuring permanent entries (expires_at="") are never deleted.
WHY: Empty string sorts BEFORE any ISO timestamp lexicographically; an unguarded expires_at < now query would delete permanent entries.

- AC2: Permanent entries (ttl_days=0, expires_at="") are NEVER deleted
- AC3: Uses ChromaDB where filter: {"$and": [{"ttl_days": {"$gt": 0}}, {"expires_at": {"$lt": current_iso}}]}
- AC4: Reports count of expired entries removed

TEST CASES (TDD):

- test_expire_preserves_permanent: insert permanent chunk (ttl_days=0), verify survives
- test_expire_preserves_future: insert chunk with future expires_at, verify survives
- test_expire_guard_empty_string: verify empty string expires_at not treated as expired

title: Fix T3 expire guard in nexus/CLAUDE.md: 2-condition guard missing expires_at check
description: Audit finding: spec.md is already correct (3-condition guard). The error is in the project-level CLAUDE.md (nexus/CLAUDE.md). The line 'T3 expire guard: always filter ttl_days > 0 AND expires_at < now' uses the 2-condition form that omits the crucial expires_at \!= '' guard. title: nexus: [Fix B] Expand AST language coverage and reconcile _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE
description:
Expand src/nexus/chunker.py:AST_EXTENSIONS from 16 to a curated subset of arcaneum's extensions. Reconcile with src/nexus/indexer.py:_EXT_TO_LANGUAGE (23 entries).

CORRECTION: Current AST_EXTENSIONS has 16 entries, not 'up to 53 from arcaneum'. Current _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE has 23 entries. The delta (in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but NOT in AST_EXTENSIONS) is: .cc, .kt, .m, .php, .r, .scala, .swift (7 extensions).

Pre-implementation step: Create a decision table of which extensions to add/exclude with justification. Extensions to EXCLUDE from AST_EXTENSIONS: .md, .yaml, .yml, .json, .toml, .html (handled by prose pipeline).

Current state (verified by code inspection):
- chunker.py:AST_EXTENSIONS has 16 entries (lines 11-28): .bash, .c, .cpp, .cs, .go, .h, .hpp, .java, .js, .jsx, .py, .rb, .rs, .sh, .ts, .tsx
- indexer.py:_EXT_TO_LANGUAGE has 23 entries (lines 28-52)
- In _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but NOT in AST_EXTENSIONS: .cc (cpp), .kt (kotlin), .m (objc), .php (php), .r (r), .scala (scala), .swift (swift)

For each new extension:
- Verify against tree-sitter-language-pack.get_parser() before adding
- Handle .m (Objective-C): maps to 'objc' in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but tree-sitter-language-pack uses 'objective-c' as the name, or may not have it — verify and remove from _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE if unsupported
- .cc maps to cpp — add to AST_EXTENSIONS
- .kt (kotlin) — verify parser availability; if available, add
- .scala — verify parser; if available, add
- .swift — verify parser; if available, add
- .php — verify parser; if available, add
- .r — verify parser; if available, add

Also consider from arcaneum's LANGUAGE_MAP: .cxx (cpp), .kts (kotlin), .sc (scala), .zsh (bash) — verify each before adding.

Post-implementation reconciliation rules:
- Every extension in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE should also be in AST_EXTENSIONS (or documented exception)
- _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE may have additional entries for comment-char selection in nexus-xtiq (Fix 1)

Validation: Fix E test_chunker_ast_languages.py must pass before AND after Fix B.

References: RDR-015 Fix B, R3 G3/G4
notes:
## Implementation Details

### Files to change:
1. src/nexus/chunker.py (lines 11-28: AST_EXTENSIONS)
2. src/nexus/indexer.py (lines 28-52: _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE)

#### Pre-implementation: Decision table
Create a decision table before writing code. For each of arcaneum's 53 extensions:
- Include in AST_EXTENSIONS? (Y/N with justification)
- Include in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE? (Y/N)
- tree-sitter-language-pack parser available? (test with get_parser())

Extensions to EXCLUDE from AST_EXTENSIONS:
- .md, .markdown — prose pipeline (SemanticMarkdownChunker)
- .yaml, .yml — config files, not code
- .json — config/data files
- .toml — config files
- .html, .htm — handled differently or not indexed
- .xml — config/data
- .csv — data files
- .sql — debatable (add if tree-sitter has a parser, otherwise skip)

Extensions to ADD (verify parser exists first):
- .kt already in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but NOT in AST_EXTENSIONS -> add
- .swift already in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but NOT in AST_EXTENSIONS -> add
- .scala already in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but NOT in AST_EXTENSIONS -> add
- .php already in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but NOT in AST_EXTENSIONS -> add
- .r — in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE, but tree-sitter-language-pack may not have R parser -> check
- .m (Objective-C) — in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE but no tree-sitter parser in the pack -> REMOVE from _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE or flag
- .cxx — in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE (maps to cpp), add to AST_EXTENSIONS as cpp
- .kts (Kotlin script) — in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE, check parser availability
- .sc (Scala script) — in _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE, check parser availability

New extensions from arcaneum to consider:
- .lua, .zig, .dart, .ex/.exs (Elixir), .hs (Haskell), .ml (OCaml), .fs (F#)
- Only add if tree-sitter-language-pack has the parser

#### Implementation in chunker.py
Expand AST_EXTENSIONS dict. For languages where tree-sitter-language-pack uses a different name than the extension implies (e.g., .cs -> 'c_sharp'), match the existing naming convention.

```python
# Verify each extension works:
# python -c 'from tree_sitter_language_pack import get_parser; get_parser("kotlin")'
```

#### Reconciliation in indexer.py
After expanding AST_EXTENSIONS, ensure _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE covers the same set (plus any comment-char-only entries). Remove .m entry or mark it with a comment explaining no tree-sitter parser is available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decision table documented (which extensions added/excluded and why)
- [ ] AST_EXTENSIONS expanded from 16 to curated subset (likely 25-35 extensions)
- [ ] _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE reconciled — every AST_EXTENSIONS entry has a corresponding _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE entry
- [ ] .m (Objective-C) handled — removed or flagged
- [ ] .cxx, .kts, .sc entries reconciled
- [ ] Each new extension verified against get_parser() before adding
- [ ] Fix E test_chunker_ast_languages.py passes before AND after this change
- [ ] Kotlin (.kt), Swift (.swift), Scala (.scala), PHP (.php) files are AST-chunked (not line-based) in a test
- [ ] pytest tests/ passes with no regressions

## Gotchas
- tree-sitter-language-pack 0.7.1 may not have parsers for all languages. Test each one.
- chunker.py AST_EXTENSIONS uses 'tsx' as language name for .tsx files, while indexer.py _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE uses 'typescript'. These serve different purposes — AST_EXTENSIONS is for CodeSplitter (needs tree-sitter language name), _EXT_TO_LANGUAGE is for the comment-char and context extraction.
- Some languages like Scala need 'scala' as the tree-sitter language name. Verify.
- The .r extension may not have a parser — R is not commonly supported by tree-sitter.

title: Fix subprocess.TimeoutExpired not caught in frecency.py — aborts entire indexing run
description: frecency.py:12-32: _git_commit_timestamps uses subprocess.run(..., timeout=30) but only checks result.returncode \!= 0. subprocess.TimeoutExpired is not caught and propagates through compute_frecency() → _run_index() → index_repository()'s except clause, which sets status='error'. A single slow git log on a locked repo or NFS mount aborts the entire indexing run. Fix: catch subprocess.TimeoutExpired inside _git_commit_timestamps, log a warning, and return []. Add a test that patches subprocess.run to raise TimeoutExpired and asserts compute_frecency returns 0.0.

title: Fix T2 single SQLite connection with unguarded reads — not thread-safe
description:
t2.py:75 uses check_same_thread=False with a single connection. The lock guards only writes (put, decay_project, restore_project, delete, expire). Read operations (get, search, list_entries, get_all, search_glob) access self.conn without any lock. The polling thread and Flask/Waitress request threads share the same T2Database instance, creating concurrent cursor state corruption risk under load.

Location: src/nexus/db/t2.py:75, 105, 143 (and all read methods)

Fix (simple): Extend self._lock to cover all conn.execute() calls. Fix (better): threading.local() with one connection per thread matching SQLite's recommended multi-threaded pattern.

title: Fix --no-rerank using simple truncation instead of round-robin interleave
description:
search_cmd.py:118-119: results = results[:n] on the --no-rerank path. round_robin_interleave is imported at line 18 but never called. title: Fix T3 collection name collision for repos sharing a basename
description:
registry.py:29 generates f"code__{repo.name}" using only the directory basename. Two repos at different paths with the same name (/work/A/myservice and /personal/myservice) both get collection code__myservice, silently polluting each other's indexed chunks. The ripgrep cache already uses a path-hash suffix (indexer.py:68) but T3 does not.

Fix: Append 8-char path hash: f"code__{repo.name}-{hashlib.sha256(str(repo).encode()).hexdigest()[:8]}". Must be applied consistently in RepoRegistry.add() and tested for idempotency on re-register.

title: Fix SessionEnd data loss: finally t1.clear() wipes unflushed entries on T2 failure

title: Implement full code chunk metadata schema per spec; fix frecency_score never stored
description: indexer.py:81-90: The spec defines 18 metadata fields for code chunks (file_path, filename, file_extension, programming_language, git_project_name, git_branch, git_commit_hash, git_remote_url, line_start, line_end, chunk_index, chunk_count, ast_chunked, has_functions, has_classes, has_imports, frecency_score, embedding_model). The indexer stores none of them — only title, tags (see C1), category, store_type, session_id, source_agent, ttl_days. Most consequential: frecency_score is never stored, so apply_hybrid_scoring() reads r.metadata.get('frecency_score', 0.0) = 0 for all code results, making --hybrid scoring effectively 0.7*vector + 0.0. The frecency score IS computed (_score variable in the loop); it just needs to be stored. Fix: expand metadata dict with all spec fields. Implement git_branch/commit/remote_url via git commands in _run_index or a helper. Requires C1 (tags fix) first.

title: nexus: [Fix C] Context prefix injection for prose and PDF pipelines
description:
Apply the same embed-only context prefix pattern from RDR-014 Fix 1 to prose (markdown) and PDF pipelines in src/nexus/indexer.py.

CORRECTION on line numbers:
- _index_prose_file() starts at line 314 (correct)
- Markdown chunk loop is at lines 372-402 (not 362-401 as stated)
- section_title assignment at line 391 reads chunk.metadata.get('header_path', '') — this is the STORED field name 'section_title', but the chunk metadata key is 'header_path'. Use chunk.metadata.get('header_path', '') directly.
- _embed_with_fallback call is at line 444 (correct)
- _index_pdf_file() starts at line 459 (correct)
- PDF title overwrite at line 510 (correct)
- PDF _embed_with_fallback call at line 522 (correct)

Implementation in _index_prose_file() — markdown path:
- Read header_path directly from chunk.metadata.get('header_path', '') — NOT from stored section_title
- Embed prefix: '## Section: {header_path}\n\n{chunk_text}' if header_path else raw text
- Build two lists: embed_texts (prefixed) -> pass to _embed_with_fallback; documents (raw) -> pass to upsert

Implementation in _index_pdf_file() — PDF path:
- source_title comes from metadatas_raw[i].get('source_title', '') — populated by _pdf_chunks at doc_indexer.py:251 from result.metadata.get('pdf_title', '')
- page_number from metadatas_raw[i].get('page_number', 0)
- Embed prefix: '## Document: {source_title}  Page: {page_number}\n\n{chunk_text}'
- Same two-list pattern

GOTCHA (confirmed): indexer.py line 510 overwrites 'title' in the augmented metadata. Use 'source_title' from metadatas_raw (before augmentation), NOT from augmented metadatas.

NOTE: The non-markdown prose path (lines 403-438) does NOT need prefix injection — those files are line-chunked without section structure.

After implementation: re-index all docs__, rdr__, and pdf__ collections with --force.

References: RDR-015 Fix C, R7, R9
notes:
## Implementation Details

### File to change: src/nexus/indexer.py

#### Change 1: _index_prose_file() — markdown path (lines 362-401)

After building the chunks list (line 367) and before the embed call (line 444):

```python
# Build two lists: embed_texts (prefixed) and documents (raw)
embed_texts: list[str] = []
# ... existing documents list already being built

for chunk in chunks:
    # Read header_path from chunk metadata (stored as section_title at line 391)
    header_path = chunk.metadata.get('header_path', '')
    
    # Build embed prefix
    if header_path:
        embed_text = f'## Section: {header_path}\n\n{chunk.text}'
    else:
        embed_text = chunk.text
    embed_texts.append(embed_text)
    documents.append(chunk.text)  # raw text for ChromaDB storage
```

Change the embed call at line 444:
```python
# BEFORE:
embeddings, actual_model = _embed_with_fallback(documents, target_model, voyage_key)

# AFTER:
embeddings, actual_model = _embed_with_fallback(embed_texts, target_model, voyage_key)
```

The non-markdown prose path (lines 403-438) does not need prefix injection — those files are line-chunked without section structure.

#### Change 2: _index_pdf_file() — PDF path (lines 459-534)

After extracting prepared tuples (line 500-503) and before the embed call (line 522):

```python
# Build embed_texts with prefix, keep documents raw
embed_texts: list[str] = []
documents_for_embed: list[str] = []

for i, (doc, meta) in enumerate(zip(documents, metadatas)):
    source_title = meta.get('source_title', '')  # from _pdf_chunks at doc_indexer.py:251
    page_number = meta.get('page_number', 0)
    
    # Build embed prefix
    prefix_parts = []
    if source_title:
        prefix_parts.append(f'Document: {source_title}')
    prefix_parts.append(f'Page: {page_number}')
    prefix = '## ' + '  '.join(prefix_parts)
    
    embed_texts.append(f'{prefix}\n\n{doc}')

# Change the embed call at line 522:
embeddings, actual_model = _embed_with_fallback(embed_texts, target_model, voyage_key)
```

CRITICAL: The 'title' field in metadatas is OVERWRITTEN at line 510 with a file-path-based string. The PDF title for the prefix must come from 'source_title' in the metadatas_raw dict (set by _pdf_chunks at doc_indexer.py:251, which reads result.metadata.get('pdf_title', '')).

#### After implementation
Re-index all docs__, rdr__, and pdf__ collections with --force.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Markdown chunks: embed_texts contains '## Section: {header_path}\n\n{text}', documents contains raw text
- [ ] PDF chunks: embed_texts contains '## Document: {title}  Page: {N}\n\n{text}', documents contains raw text
- [ ] Non-markdown prose files are unchanged (no prefix)
- [ ] _embed_with_fallback receives embed_texts for both prose and PDF
- [ ] upsert_chunks_with_embeddings receives documents (raw) for both
- [ ] source_title used (not overwritten title) for PDF prefix
- [ ] After --force re-index, Fix 2 dedup remains clean (no repeated sentences)
- [ ] pytest tests/ passes

## Gotchas
- The title field at indexer.py:510 is NOT the PDF title — it is overwritten with f'{file.relative_to(repo)}:page-{page_number}'. The PDF title is in source_title (from doc_indexer.py:251).
- header_path may be empty for content before the first heading in a markdown file. In that case, use raw text as embed_text (no prefix).
- The non-markdown prose path (line-chunked .txt/.rst etc.) has no section structure metadata, so no prefix injection is applied there.
- embed_texts and documents must be the same length. Filter them together if any documents are empty.

title: Fix semantic chunker never writing chunk_start_char/chunk_end_char
description:
md_chunker.py:223-240 (_make_chunk) never writes chunk_start_char or chunk_end_char metadata fields. doc_indexer.py:171-172 defaults both to 0 then adds frontmatter_len. Result: every semantically-chunked Markdown doc stores chunk_start_char=frontmatter_len and chunk_end_char=frontmatter_len for every chunk — a zero-width range at the frontmatter boundary. All character offsets are wrong for the primary (semantic) indexing path.

Location: src/nexus/md_chunker.py:223-240, src/nexus/doc_indexer.py:171-172

Fix: Track character positions in _build_sections using token.map (line ranges) combined with a precomputed per-line char offset table from source_text. Pass start_char/end_char to _make_chunk as optional params. The naive fallback path already emits correct offsets (md_chunker.py:260-261).

title: RDR-017 Phase 1c/1d: Add on_start/on_file callback hooks to indexer.py and doc_indexer.py
description:
## Context

RDR-017 Phase 1c/1d: Add two-hook progress interface to the indexing layer.
The CLI (Phase 2) will use these hooks to drive tqdm bars without any tqdm
import in the indexer itself.

**Depends on:** nexus-wvys (helpers must return int before on_file can report chunks)

## Two-hook interface

```python
on_start: Callable[[int], None] | None = None
# Fires once before iteration with total file count.
# CLI uses this to create the tqdm bar with the correct total=.

on_file: Callable[[Path, int, float], None] | None = None
# Fires after each file: (file_path, chunks_produced, elapsed_s).
# chunks=0 means skipped or failed.
```

## TWO SEPARATE WIRING PATHS — keep them separate

### Path A: Repository indexing (index_repo_cmd → index_repository → _run_index)

`on_start` and `on_file` are wired through `index_repository()` → `_run_index()`.

`_run_index()` fires these hooks. The hooks cover only:
`code_files + prose_files + pdf_files` — NOT RDR files.

RDR files are processed by `_discover_and_index_rdrs()` which runs AFTER the
main loop. RDR files are NOT countable up front (their count is discovered
inside `_discover_and_index_rdrs`). Therefore:
- `_discover_and_index_rdrs()` does NOT receive `on_file`
- `batch_index_markdowns()` when called from `_discover_and_index_rdrs` does NOT receive `on_file`
- RDR file progress is reported separately via the existing `click.echo("  RDR documents: ...")`

### Path B: Standalone RDR indexing (index_rdr_cmd → batch_index_markdowns)

`index_rdr_cmd` calls `batch_index_markdowns` directly (NOT via `_discover_and_index_rdrs`).
The Phase 2 CLI bead (nexus-p0me) needs `batch_index_markdowns` to accept `on_file` so
it can update the tqdm bar from `index_rdr_cmd`.

Similarly, `batch_index_pdfs` accepts `on_file` for use by any future CLI caller.

These callbacks in `batch_index_markdowns`/`batch_index_pdfs` are ONLY for
the standalone CLI path. They do not interact with the `_run_index` callback path.

## What to do

**TDD — write failing tests first.**

### Test file: tests/test_indexer.py

Add tests:
1. `on_start` called exactly once with `len(code_files) + len(prose_files) + len(pdf_files)`
   (count does NOT include RDR files)
2. `on_file` called exactly once per non-RDR file with correct `(Path, int, float)` signature
3. `chunks=0` (int) emitted for skipped files (staleness check passes, file not re-indexed)
4. `on_start=None` and `on_file=None` are safe defaults — all existing callers unaffected
5. `elapsed_s` is a non-negative float for each `on_file` call
6. `_discover_and_index_rdrs` is NOT passed `on_file` — it fires zero `on_file` callbacks

### Test file: tests/test_doc_indexer.py

Add tests:
1. `batch_index_markdowns` calls `on_file(path, chunks, elapsed_s)` after each markdown
2. `batch_index_pdfs` calls `on_file(path, chunks, elapsed_s)` after each PDF
3. `on_file=None` is a safe default for both batch functions

### Implementation: src/nexus/indexer.py

**`index_repository()`** — add `on_start` and `on_file` kwargs, thread to `_run_index()`.

**`_run_index()`**:
- Add `on_start: Callable[[int], None] | None = None`
  and `on_file: Callable[[Path, int, float], None] | None = None`
- After building `code_files`, `prose_files`, `pdf_files` lists:
  ```python
  if on_start:
      on_start(len(code_files) + len(prose_files) + len(pdf_files))
  ```
- Per file in all three loops (code, prose, pdf):
  ```python
  t0 = time.monotonic()
  chunks = _index_code_file(...)  # returns int (Phase 1a)
  elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
  if on_file:
      on_file(path, chunks, elapsed)
  ```
- `_discover_and_index_rdrs()` is called AFTER the main loops and does NOT
  receive `on_file`. Do not thread the callback into it.

**`_discover_and_index_rdrs()`** — NO changes needed for callback wiring.

### Implementation: src/nexus/doc_indexer.py

**`batch_index_markdowns()`** — add `on_file: Callable[[Path, int, float], None] | None = None`:
```python
for path in paths:
    t0 = time.monotonic()
    chunks = index_markdown(path, ...)
    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
    if on_file:
        on_file(path, chunks, elapsed)
```

**`batch_index_pdfs()`** — same pattern.

Note: `time` is already imported in both files.

## Acceptance criteria

- `on_start` fires exactly once with correct total (code+prose+pdf, NOT RDR count)
- `on_file` fires once per non-RDR file with `(Path, int, float)`
- RDR files do NOT trigger `on_file` calls
- `None` defaults work — all existing callers unaffected
- `batch_index_markdowns` and `batch_index_pdfs` call `on_file` per-file (for standalone CLI path)
- All new tests pass; all existing tests pass

## Files

- `src/nexus/indexer.py`
- `src/nexus/doc_indexer.py`
- `tests/test_indexer.py`
- `tests/test_doc_indexer.py`
close_reason: on_start/on_file hooks implemented with TDD, 9 new tests, 1829 total pass

title: Phase 2b: Add byte cap post-pass to md_chunker.py
Fix oversized content parts in md_chunker can produce chunks exceeding embedding context window
description: md_chunker.py:195-218: In _split_large_section, when a single part (e.g. a large fenced code block) exceeds chunk_size tokens, it is set as current_parts without further splitting. At the end of the loop this oversized chunk is flushed as-is. For Voyage AI's context window, a code block of ~1700+ chars (chunk_size=512) exceeds the limit and may be silently truncated or rejected. Fix: add sub-splitting of individual parts that exceed chunk_size, using the same naive line-splitting approach as the overall fallback. Add a test creating a section with a code block larger than chunk_size and asserting all output chunks are within the limit.

title: Fix T2Database thread safety: missing check_same_thread=False and lock
description: db/t2.py:79: T2Database opens SQLite with default check_same_thread=True. Sharing a T2Database instance across threads raises ProgrammingError: SQLite objects created in a thread can only be used in that same thread. Current CLI commands each create a new T2Database per invocation so this doesn't fire today, but the moment any Flask server route shares a T2Database singleton (natural extension path), every concurrent request fails. Fix: add check_same_thread=False to sqlite3.connect() and add a threading.Lock to serialize all conn.execute() calls. WAL mode allows concurrent readers from separate connections but a single shared connection still needs write serialization.

title: Add missing spec-defined search flags: --where, context lines, --reverse, -m
description:
Add missing spec-defined search flags: --where, context lines (-A/-B/-C), --reverse, -m.

WHY: These flags are defined in spec.md but absent from search_cmd.py, leaving the search CLI materially incomplete.

FLAGS TO ADD:
- --where KEY=VALUE  — metadata filter passed to ChromaDB where clause (can repeat: --where lang=python --where has_tests=true)
- -A N  — show N lines of context after each code match (stored context from chunk)
- -B N  — show N lines of context before each code match
- -C N  — shorthand for -A N -B N (show N lines above and below)
- --reverse  — reverse output order (highest-scoring last, useful for piping to tail)
- -m N  — alias for --max-results N (spec lists both; -m is the short form)

CRITICAL: FLAG COLLISION — -C flag conflict
The current implementation uses -C/--corpus as a short flag (search_cmd.py line ~26).
The spec defines -C N as context-lines shorthand. This is a direct collision.
Resolution BEFORE implementation required:
  Option A: Keep -C for context (spec intent), rename --corpus short flag to something else
  Option B: Drop -C context shorthand, use only -A/-B; document deviation from spec
  The spec takes -C for context; preferred resolution is Option A with corpus having no short flag or using a different letter.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- --where KEY=VALUE filters results by metadata field
- -A/-B/-C context flags work for results that have context stored in chunk metadata
- --reverse reverses result ordering
- -m is accepted as alias for --max-results

title: Enforce 16KB chunk size limit before ChromaDB upsert
description:
## Problem

ChromaDB enforces a 16KB document size limit per chunk (embedding window constraint).
When a chunk exceeds this, the upsert fails with:
  ChromaError: Quota exceeded: 'Document size (bytes)' exceeded quota limit for action 'Upsert'

Example failure: ART reindex (bfdo9z49j) failed because one chunk was 17576 bytes vs 16384 limit.

## Root Cause

Nexus does not validate chunk size before calling ChromaDB. Large files or chunks
produced by the AST/semantic/PDF chunkers can exceed 16384 bytes if the underlying
content is dense (minified code, long docstrings, large prose paragraphs, etc.).

## Required Fix

Add a pre-upsert size guard in the ChromaDB write path (T3). Options:
1. In `_write_batch` (t3.py): drop or truncate any chunk whose document byte length > 16384,
   log a warning with the source_path and chunk index.
2. Alternative: enforce in each chunker's output — but that's 3 separate places.

Recommended approach: single guard in `t3.py:_write_batch` — one fix covers all paths
(code, prose, PDF, RDR). Log at WARNING level with source_path so callers can see
which file triggered it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Chunks > 16384 bytes are silently dropped (with WARNING log) before upsert
- ART can be reindexed without quota errors
- Unit test: _write_batch drops oversized chunks and logs warning
- Existing upsert tests still pass

## Files

- `src/nexus/db/t3.py` (_write_batch method)
- `tests/test_t3.py` (or nearest T3 test file)

title: Fix chunk_start_char/chunk_end_char offsets wrong for documents with frontmatter
description: doc_indexer.py:132-139: The chunker receives 'body' (frontmatter stripped) but stores chunk_start_char/chunk_end_char as offsets into body. title: Fix decay_project/restore_project tag replacement silent no-op when tags='pm'
description: db/t2.py:222-244: Both methods rely on SQL replace(tags, 'pm,', 'pm-archived,'). The pattern requires a trailing comma after 'pm'. When tags='pm' (last or only tag) or tags='phase:1,pm' (pm is last), the replacement silently fails. The entry stays tagged 'pm' after decay — the T2 decay phase of pm_archive is effectively a no-op for these entries. Fix: use SQL CASE expression to handle the boundary: WHEN tags='pm' THEN 'pm-archived', WHEN tags LIKE '%,pm' THEN replace(tags, ',pm', ',pm-archived'), ELSE replace(tags, 'pm,', 'pm-archived,'). Apply the inverse pattern to restore_project. Add direct unit tests for both methods covering pm-only, pm-last, and pm-first tag positions.

Fix TOCTOU permissions window on credential file writes
description:
config.py:120-121 performs os.replace(tmp_path, path) then os.chmod(path, 0o600). Between those two lines the credential file is live at its final path with umask-default permissions (typically 0o644 or 0o640). A concurrent reader in that window can read credentials. A process kill between lines 120 and 121 leaves the file permanently world-readable.

Location: src/nexus/config.py:120-121

Fix: Move os.chmod(tmp_path, 0o600) to BEFORE os.replace() — secure the temp file first, then atomically rename. Remove the os.chmod(path, ...) call that was on line 121.

title: Fix pm_archive col.update() wipes store_type and other required metadata fields
description: pm.py:351-372: t3.put() stores chunk metadata including store_type='pm-archive', title, ttl_days=0, expires_at=''. The subsequent col.update(ids=[doc_id], metadatas=[update_meta]) replaces the entire metadata (ChromaDB update semantics = full replacement, not merge). update_meta contains only project/status/archived_at/phase_count/pm_doc_count/pm_latest_timestamp. Consequence: (1) store_type is erased so the idempotency filter never matches — every pm_archive call re-synthesizes and appends a duplicate to T3. (2) ttl_days and expires_at are wiped. Fix: merge update_meta into the full metadata dict before calling col.update(), or pass all fields to t3.put() directly and remove the separate col.update() call.

title: Fix path traversal in DELETE /repos/<path> — no canonicalization of route parameter
description: server.py:76-83: Flask's <path:> converter accepts any non-empty string including '..' components. full_path = Path('/' + repo_path) is not canonicalized. A crafted path like DELETE /repos/home/user/../../etc/sensitive produces an unnormalized path. While no file I/O occurs here (only registry lookup), consistently resolving paths at both add and delete time ensures key matching is correct and establishes safe patterns for future expansion. Fix: full_path = Path('/' + repo_path).resolve(). Also ensure RepoRegistry.add() stores resolved paths.

title: feat: --force flag on nx index repo/pdf/md/rdr
description: Add --force flag to all four nx index subcommands (repo, pdf, md, rdr) to bypass the content_hash+embedding_model staleness check. Without --force, re-indexing after logic changes (e.g. RDR-016 context prefix fix) requires deleting the entire collection. With --force, files are re-chunked and re-embedded in-place via upsert, preserving collection availability. Follows frecency_only pattern in indexer.py. Affects: _index_code_file(), _index_doc_file(), _index_pdf_file(), batch_index_markdowns(); index_repository(); commands/index.py all four Click commands.

title: Fix pm_archive idempotency check: replace Voyage AI embedding call with metadata-only filter
description: pm.py:310-324: The idempotency check calls t3.search('Archive', [collection], n_results=1, where=...) which embeds the word 'Archive' via Voyage AI API just to check whether a synthesis already exists. This costs an API round-trip for a presence check, may return false positives, and creates the collection as a side effect. Fix: use col.get(where={'store_type': {'': 'pm-archive'}}, include=['metadatas'], limit=1) which is a pure metadata filter with no embedding call. Also note: this fix is dependent on C5 (col.update wipes store_type) being fixed first, otherwise store_type won't be present in metadata.

title: Fix nx config get prints raw credential to stdout
description: commands/config_cmd.py:67-71: 'nx config get' emits the full plaintext secret. nx config list uses _mask() but get does not. This value ends up in shell history, terminal scrollback, and log files. Fix: add a --raw flag that defaults to masked output for interactive use. Or at minimum document that this command is intended for scripting use and prints unmasked output.

title: Fix pm_unblock silent no-op then prints success message for out-of-range line
description: pm.py:232-234: When line is 0 or greater than the number of blockers, bullets.pop(idx) is skipped silently. unblock_cmd then prints 'Blocker N removed.' even though no blocker was removed. Fix: raise IndexError or ValueError from pm_unblock when idx is out of range; catch in unblock_cmd with an informative ClickException and non-zero exit. Add a test verifying the error for line=0 and line=999 with only 2 blockers.

title: Remove false scratch PRODUCE sections from agents

These sections are documentation debt. Remove or simplify scratch PRODUCE instructions from: plan-auditor, deep-research-synthesizer, code-review-expert, java-developer, java-debugger, deep-analyst. Keep the codebase-deep-analyzer pattern as it is the only real use case.
close_reason: Superseded by RDR-010: once T1 scratch is backed by SQLite with session ID propagation, subagent scratch sharing will work as documented. No cleanup needed.

title: Fix _ef_cache in T3Database not thread-safe under concurrent requests
description:
t3.py:40 defines self._ef_cache: dict[str, object] = {} with no lock. Two threads hitting a cache miss on the same collection name both construct a VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction (wasteful, two API handshakes). The dict itself can be in inconsistent state during concurrent modification in CPython. Currently safe because Waitress threads=1, but will race if threading expands.

Location: src/nexus/db/t3.py:40, 53-58

Fix: Add self._ef_lock = threading.Lock(). Use double-checked locking in _embedding_fn(): check cache, acquire lock, check again, then construct and store. Return cached value holding the lock on write, release immediately.

source = b'''
class MyClass:
    def my_method(self):
        x = 1
        y = 2

'''
cls, method = _extract_context(source, 'python', 2, 4)  # 0-indexed lines

2. test_extract_context_java:

assert cls == ''

4. test_extract_context_multi_method_span:
```python

source = b'''
class Foo:
    def bar(self): pass
    def baz(self): pass
'''
cls, method = _extract_context(source, 'python', 2, 3)
assert cls == 'Foo'
assert method == ''  # spans two methods, neither fully contains chunk

title: RDR-016: Fix AST chunk line ranges using node.start_char_idx
description: Fix chunk_file() in src/nexus/chunker.py lines 208-214. Replace the broken setdefault fallback (which assigns every AST chunk line_start=1, line_end=total_lines) with character-offset-derived line ranges using node.start_char_idx. This is a TextNode attribute that IS accurately populated. Formula: line_start = content[:node.start_char_idx].count('\n') + 1; line_end = line_start + len(node.text.splitlines()) - 1. title: Fix promote_cmd never computing expires_at — all promoted entries become permanent
description:
memory.py:122-123 passes only ttl_days to t3.put(); expires_at is never computed. T3 expires() guards on expires_at != '' — since it is always '' for promoted entries, T3 expiry NEVER fires for any promoted entry, regardless of ttl_days. Every T2 entry with a finite TTL silently becomes permanent in T3.

Location: src/nexus/commands/memory.py:122-131

Fix: When ttl_days > 0, compute expires_at = (datetime.fromisoformat(entry['timestamp']) + timedelta(days=ttl_days)).isoformat() and pass to t3.put(). When ttl is None (permanent), pass ttl_days=0 and expires_at='' as-is.

title: Fix pm_archive multi-chunk partial write can cause incomplete archive to look complete
description: pm.py:345-375: When synthesis > 3960 chars produces multiple chunks, the write loop calls t3.put()+col.update() per chunk. If chunk k succeeds but chunk k+1 fails, T3 has a partial synthesis. The next pm_archive call's idempotency check may retrieve chunk k, find matching pm_doc_count+pm_latest_timestamp, and treat the partial archive as complete — then decay T2. Fix: generate a synthesis_id UUID before the write loop and store it in every chunk's metadata. Add a chunk_total field. The idempotency check should verify that all chunks with matching synthesis_id are present (count == chunk_total) before treating the archive as complete.

title: Fix T1 search returning results from all sessions (not session-scoped)
description:
T1Database.search() calls self._col.query() without a where={session_id: self._session_id} filter. The shared collection contains all sessions' scratch data, so Session A's entries are visible from Session B's nx scratch search. Stale entries from crashed sessions also pollute results.

Location: src/nexus/db/t1.py (search method)

Fix: Add where={'session_id': self._session_id} to every T1Database query. Note: ChromaDB n_results must not exceed filtered count, so update n_results = min(n_results, filtered_count) logic. If C1 (EphemeralClient) is fixed first with per-session clients, this issue dissolves naturally.

title: Enhance nx serve status with accuracy % and uptime display
description: spec.md line ~753 specifies nx serve status should show: indexed repos, accuracy %, and uptime. Actual implementation (commands/serve.py status_cmd) shows PID and repo list only — no accuracy percentage, no server uptime. title: nexus: [Fix C2] preserve_code_blocks option for SemanticMarkdownChunker
description:
Add preserve_code_blocks: bool = True constructor parameter to SemanticMarkdownChunker in src/nexus/md_chunker.py.

CORRECTION: The arcaneum reference at lines 274-361 of arcaneum/src/arcaneum/indexing/markdown/chunker.py is the correct location for the source pattern (verified). The arcaneum chunker has preserve_code_blocks support with is_code_block detection already in _split_large_section (lines 274-361). The nexus implementation must be adapted, not a direct copy, because the nexus class structure differs (uses _make_chunk not _create_chunk, uses _CHARS_PER_TOKEN not CHARS_PER_TOKEN).

Implementation:
- In SemanticMarkdownChunker.__init__ (md_chunker.py:65-74): add preserve_code_blocks parameter. The existing nexus code does NOT have this parameter — confirm by inspection of line 65.
- is_code_block is already tracked per content_part via the 'is_code_block' key in _build_sections (line 164): token.type in ('code_block', 'fence')
- In _split_large_section (lines 231-289): add logic that when self.preserve_code_blocks and part['is_code_block'] and adding would exceed budget: flush current_parts first, then emit the code block atomically
- Add has_code_blocks: bool to chunk metadata in _make_chunk or at call site

NOTE: The nexus _make_chunk method (line 291) does not currently accept has_code_blocks. Either add it as a keyword argument or compute it from the text at the call site.

Tests driven by test_md_chunker_semantic_integrity.py (Fix E bead nexus-0qnh):
- Test that a code block within a section is not split across chunks
- Test that has_code_blocks metadata is True when chunk contains a fenced code block

~20 LOC.

References: RDR-015 Fix C2, R7 G9/G10
notes:
## Implementation Details

### File to change: src/nexus/md_chunker.py

#### Change 1: Constructor (lines 65-74)
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    chunk_size: int = 512,
    chunk_overlap: int = 50,
    preserve_code_blocks: bool = True,  # NEW
) -> None:
    self.chunk_size = chunk_size
    self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap
    self.preserve_code_blocks = preserve_code_blocks  # NEW
    self.max_chars = int(chunk_size * _CHARS_PER_TOKEN)
    self.overlap_chars = int(chunk_overlap * _CHARS_PER_TOKEN)
    self.md = MarkdownIt() if MARKDOWN_IT_AVAILABLE else None
```

#### Change 2: _split_large_section (lines 231-289)
When self.preserve_code_blocks is True and a content_part has is_code_block=True, treat it as an atomic unit — do not split it even if it exceeds the token budget for the current chunk. Instead:
- If current_parts is non-empty and adding the code block would exceed the limit, flush current_parts as a chunk first
- Then add the code block as part of a new chunk (or as its own chunk if it alone exceeds the limit)

```python
for part in section['content_parts']:
    part_text = part['content']
    part_tokens = len(part_text) / _CHARS_PER_TOKEN
    is_code = part.get('is_code_block', False)
    
    if self.preserve_code_blocks and is_code and current_tokens + part_tokens > self.chunk_size:
        # Flush current chunk before the code block
        if current_parts:
            chunks.append(self._make_chunk(...))
            chunk_index += 1
        # Start new chunk with the code block (even if it exceeds size)
        current_parts = [header_text, part_text] if header_text else [part_text]
        current_tokens = len(part_text) / _CHARS_PER_TOKEN + header_tokens
        continue
    
    # ... existing logic for non-code parts
```

#### Change 3: Add has_code_blocks metadata
In _make_chunk (lines 291-312), detect if any content_part is a code block. This requires passing the information through.

Option A: Add has_code_blocks parameter to _make_chunk:
```python
def _make_chunk(self, text, chunk_index, base_metadata, header_path,
                chunk_start_char=0, chunk_end_char=0, has_code_blocks=False):
    meta = {
        **base_metadata,
        'chunk_index': chunk_index,
        'header_path': ' > '.join(header_path) if header_path else '',
        'chunk_start_char': chunk_start_char,
        'chunk_end_char': chunk_end_char,
        'has_code_blocks': has_code_blocks,  # NEW
    }
```

Option B: Detect code fences in the chunk text:
```python
'has_code_blocks': bool(re.search(r'^```', text, re.MULTILINE))
```

Option B is simpler and does not require plumbing is_code_block through the chunk-building path.

Source pattern: arcaneum/src/arcaneum/indexing/markdown/chunker.py:274-361
~20 LOC total

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] preserve_code_blocks=True is the default
- [ ] Fenced code blocks are not split mid-block in _split_large_section
- [ ] has_code_blocks: bool present in chunk metadata
- [ ] has_code_blocks is True for chunks containing fenced code blocks
- [ ] has_code_blocks is False for chunks without code blocks
- [ ] test_md_chunker_semantic_integrity.py (Fix E part 2) tests pass
- [ ] Existing tests in tests/test_md_chunker.py continue to pass
- [ ] pytest tests/ passes with no regressions

## Gotchas
- A fenced code block that is larger than max_chars must still be emitted (even though it exceeds the size limit). The alternative — splitting inside the code block — is worse.
- The is_code_block flag is already set in _build_sections at line 164: token.type in ('code_block', 'fence'). This flag is available in content_parts and can be used in _split_large_section.
- The 'fence' token type is for backtick-fenced blocks. 'code_block' is for indented code blocks. Both should be preserved.

title: Fix apply_hybrid_scoring inverted ranking
description: search_engine.py:43,79-87: min_max_normalize maps smallest distance (most relevant) to 0.0 and largest to 1.0. description:
## Context

RDR-017 Phase 2: Wire tqdm progress bars and --monitor flag into all four
`nx index` subcommands. The indexer callback hooks are provided by Phase 1.

**Depends on:** nexus-9c2x (on_start/on_file hooks must exist first), nexus-ph2g (tqdm dep)

## What to do

**TDD — write failing tests first.**

### File: src/nexus/commands/index.py

---

#### `index_repo_cmd`

Add `--monitor` flag:
```python
@click.option("--monitor", is_flag=True, default=False,
              help="Print per-file progress lines (verbose monitoring without debug spam).")
```

Build closures:

```python
bar = None
n = 0
total = 0

def on_start(count: int) -> None:
    nonlocal bar, total
    total = count
    bar = tqdm(total=count, disable=None, desc=path.name, unit="file")

def on_file(fpath: Path, chunks: int, elapsed: float) -> None:
    nonlocal n
    n += 1
    if bar is not None:
        bar.update(1)
        bar.set_postfix(now=fpath.name)
    if monitor:
        label = f"{chunks} chunks" if chunks else "skipped"
        line = f"  [{n}/{total}] {fpath.name} — {label}  ({elapsed:.1f}s)"
        if bar is not None and sys.stdout.isatty():
            tqdm.write(line)
        else:
            click.echo(line)
```

Pass `on_start=on_start, on_file=on_file` to `index_repository()`.
Close bar after call: `if bar: bar.close()`.

**Implementation note for non-TTY**: `n` is tracked as a counter variable
(NOT `bar.n`) because `bar.n` is unreliable when `disable=True`.

---

#### `index_rdr_cmd`

Add `--monitor` flag.

Create bar before `batch_index_markdowns`:
```python
bar = tqdm(total=len(rdr_files), disable=None, desc="RDR", unit="doc")
n = 0

def on_file(fpath: Path, chunks: int, elapsed: float) -> None:
    nonlocal n
    n += 1
    bar.update(1)
    bar.set_postfix(now=fpath.name)
    if monitor:
        label = f"{chunks} chunks" if chunks else "skipped"
        line = f"  [{n}/{len(rdr_files)}] {fpath.name} — {label}  ({elapsed:.1f}s)"
        if sys.stdout.isatty():
            tqdm.write(line)
        else:
            click.echo(line)

results = batch_index_markdowns(rdr_files, ..., on_file=on_file)
bar.close()
```

`on_start` not needed here (total known before the call from `len(rdr_files)`).

---

#### `index_pdf_cmd`

Add `--monitor` flag. No tqdm bar (single file, completes in seconds).

In the non-dry-run path, when `--monitor` is set, call with `return_metadata=True`:

```python
if monitor:
    result = index_pdf(path, ..., force=force, return_metadata=True)
    n = result["chunks"]
    pages = result.get("pages", [])
    page_range = f"{pages[0]}–{pages[-1]}" if len(pages) > 1 else str(pages[0]) if pages else "?"
    title = result.get("title", "")
    author = result.get("author", "")
    parts = [f"Chunks: {n}", f"Pages: {page_range}"]
    if title: parts.append(f'Title: "{title}"')
    if author: parts.append(f'Author: "{author}"')
    click.echo(f"\n  {'  '.join(parts)}")
else:
    n = index_pdf(path, ..., force=force)
click.echo(f"{result_label} {n} chunk(s).")
```

---

#### `index_md_cmd`

Add `--monitor` flag. No tqdm bar.

When `--monitor`, call with `return_metadata=True`:
```python
if monitor:
    result = index_markdown(path, ..., force=force, return_metadata=True)
    n = result["chunks"]
    sections = result.get("sections", 0)
    click.echo(f"\n  Chunks: {n}  Sections: {sections}")
else:
    n = index_markdown(path, ..., force=force)
click.echo(f"{result_label} {n} chunk(s).")
```

---

### Tests: tests/test_index_cmd.py

1. `--monitor` flag accepted by all four commands (exit 0)
2. `index_repo_cmd` without `--monitor`: `index_repository` called with `on_start` and `on_file` callables

## Acceptance criteria

title: nexus: [RDR-014 Fix 2] Markdown dedup blocklist in SemanticMarkdownChunker
description:
In src/nexus/md_chunker.py:_build_sections(), structural tokens (paragraph_open, list_item_open, etc.) duplicate content from their child inline tokens because the .map fallback path in _token_content() returns source lines that the inline token also returns via .content.

Add _STRUCTURAL_TOKEN_TYPES frozenset and skip those tokens when accumulating content_parts.

TDD: Write tests FIRST (test_no_duplicate_content_in_chunk, test_structural_token_not_duplicated, test_spurious_split_resolved), then implement the fix.

CORRECTION on test_spurious_split_resolved: The test as written asserts len(chunks) == 1, but the 10 paragraphs each ~300 chars sum to ~3000 chars (~909 tokens) at default chunk_size=512. After deduplication fixes, chunks may consolidate but likely produce 2 chunks, not 1. The assertion should be: assert len(chunks) < 5 (or document the expected post-fix count). Verify with actual measurements after implementation.

After implementation: re-index all docs__ and rdr__ collections with --force.

References: RDR-014 Fix 2, RDR-014 R10
notes:
## Implementation Details

### File to change: src/nexus/md_chunker.py

#### Step 1 (TDD): Write tests FIRST
Add to tests/test_md_chunker.py (existing file) OR create a new dedicated test file.

Three test functions specified in RDR-014:

```python
def test_no_duplicate_content_in_chunk():
    text = '### Section\n\nA paragraph with content.\n\n- bullet one\n- bullet two\n'
    chunks = SemanticMarkdownChunker().chunk(text, {})
    assert len(chunks) == 1
    assert chunks[0].text.count('A paragraph with content.') == 1
    assert chunks[0].text.count('bullet one') == 1

def test_structural_token_not_duplicated():
    text = 'Paragraph text.\n\nSecond paragraph.\n'
    chunks = SemanticMarkdownChunker().chunk(text, {})
    full_text = '\n'.join(c.text for c in chunks)
    assert full_text.count('Paragraph text.') == 1
    assert full_text.count('Second paragraph.') == 1

def test_spurious_split_resolved():
    sentence = 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog and continues running. '
    paragraph = sentence * 5
    text = '\n\n'.join(f'Para {i}: {paragraph}' for i in range(10))
    chunks = SemanticMarkdownChunker().chunk(text, {})
    assert len(chunks) == 1
```

#### Step 2: Implement the fix
At module level in md_chunker.py (after imports, before class):

```python
_STRUCTURAL_TOKEN_TYPES = frozenset({
    'paragraph_open', 'paragraph_close',
    'bullet_list_open', 'bullet_list_close',
    'ordered_list_open', 'ordered_list_close',
    'list_item_open', 'list_item_close',
    'blockquote_open', 'blockquote_close',
    'table_open', 'table_close',
    'thead_open', 'thead_close',
    'tbody_open', 'tbody_close',
    'tr_open', 'tr_close',
    'td_open', 'td_close',
    'th_open', 'th_close',
})
```

In _build_sections() at line 158-167, change the content accumulation:

```python
# BEFORE (line 158-167):
content = self._token_content(token, source_text)
if content:
    current_section['content_parts'].append(...)

# AFTER:
content = self._token_content(token, source_text)
if content and token.type not in _STRUCTURAL_TOKEN_TYPES:
    current_section['content_parts'].append(...)
```

#### Step 3: Re-index
After implementation: re-index all docs__ and rdr__ collections with --force.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test_no_duplicate_content_in_chunk passes
- [ ] test_structural_token_not_duplicated passes
- [ ] test_spurious_split_resolved passes
- [ ] Existing tests in tests/test_md_chunker.py continue to pass
- [ ] Full suite: pytest tests/ passes with no regressions
- [ ] _STRUCTURAL_TOKEN_TYPES is a frozenset (immutable)
- [ ] The blocklist defaults to INCLUDE for unknown future token types (new plugin tokens pass through)

## Gotchas
- strong_open/close, em_open/close, link_open/close are inline-level tokens from markdown-it-py inline rules. They do NOT appear at the top level of md.parse() output. Including them in the blocklist is harmless but unnecessary (R10).
- The _token_content method's .map fallback path is the root cause. We are NOT changing _token_content — we are filtering at the caller site in _build_sections. This is safer because _token_content might be used elsewhere.
- markdown-it-py 4.0.0 has exactly 11 block rules (stable, CommonMark-based). The blocklist is complete for the current version.

## Definition of Done
- All 3 new tests pass
- All existing tests pass
- docs__ and rdr__ collections re-indexed with --force
- nx search 'cognitive pipeline log-polar' --corpus docs__ART-8c2e74c0 -m 1 --json shows no repeated sentences in content field
